articles.title: Michael Jackson Estate Paid $15M to Erase Abuse Claims from Biopic
articles.summary: The Michael Jackson Estate spent 15 million dollars to remove all mention of child abuse allegations from the upcoming Michael Jackson biopic, according to a new Variety report. The move raises serious questions about how much control estates should have over biopics that are supposed to tell real stories.
articles.content:
The Michael Jackson biopic has been one of the most closely watched and controversial film projects in Hollywood. The Estate, which controls Jackson's legacy and earns enormous sums from his music and image, reportedly paid 15 million dollars specifically to ensure any reference to child abuse allegations was removed from the final film. According to Variety, any mention of the accusations has been completely scrubbed.

The allegations against Michael Jackson have been part of public conversation for decades. The 2019 documentary Leaving Neverland, which featured two men who said Jackson abused them as children, reignited intense debate about his legacy. The Estate has consistently and forcefully denied all accusations. But spending 15 million dollars to shape a film's content is a whole different level of involvement.

This matters because biopics are increasingly how casual audiences learn about major cultural figures. When the people who profit from an artist's legacy can pay to control the narrative in a film, what you end up with is not a biography. It is a marketing exercise. Fans deserve to know what kind of film they are actually watching before they buy a ticket.
keyPoints: The Michael Jackson Estate paid 15 million dollars to remove abuse allegations from his biopic
All mention of the accusations has reportedly been scrubbed from the film
The allegations against Jackson have been debated publicly since the 1990s
The 2019 documentary Leaving Neverland brought fresh attention to the accusations
The story raises big questions about who gets to control the narrative in celebrity biopics

articles.title: Karol G Warned to Drop 'ICE Out' at Coachella
articles.summary: Coachella headliner Karol G was reportedly warned not to say 'ICE out' during her performance or risk losing her visa to remain in the United States. The Colombian reggaeton superstar is navigating a charged political moment just days before one of the biggest sets of her career.
articles.content:
Karol G is one of the biggest names in music right now and she is headlining Coachella, the massive annual festival in California that attracts millions of viewers worldwide. But ahead of her set she has reportedly been warned to stay away from any phrase that could be read as anti-ICE. ICE stands for Immigration and Customs Enforcement, the US government agency that has been at the center of high-profile deportation controversies in 2025 and 2026.

The warning is serious because Karol G, whose real name is Carolina Giraldo Navarro, is Colombian and holds a visa to work in the United States. Saying something that authorities could interpret as politically hostile could put that status at risk. As one source put it, you become bait, because some people want to show their power.

This puts Karol G in an incredibly difficult position. She has a massive Latin fanbase, many of whom have been directly affected by immigration enforcement. Staying silent on stage might feel like a betrayal to some fans. Speaking up carries real personal risk. How she handles Sunday night will be one of the most watched moments of the festival season.
keyPoints: Karol G was warned not to say 'ICE out' at Coachella or risk losing her US visa
ICE is the US immigration enforcement agency behind recent high-profile deportations
Karol G is a Colombian artist who needs a visa to perform in the United States
She is headlining Coachella, one of the world's biggest music festivals
The situation puts her in a direct conflict between artistic freedom and personal legal risk

articles.title: Sam Levinson Dedicates Euphoria Season 3 to Lost Stars
articles.summary: Euphoria creator Sam Levinson has revealed that the show's third season is dedicated to those who died, including actor Angus Cloud and Eric Dane. The tribute adds real emotional weight to a season that has already been one of the most anticipated returns on TV.
articles.content:
Sam Levinson made it personal before Euphoria Season 3 even airs. The creator announced the eight-episode season, which debuts on HBO and Max this Sunday April 12, is dedicated to those the show has lost. That includes Angus Cloud, who played the beloved character Fez and passed away in July 2023, and Eric Dane, the actor who played Cal Jacobs and died earlier this year.

Angus Cloud's death hit the Euphoria fanbase incredibly hard. He was only 25 and had become one of the most beloved parts of the show. Eric Dane, best known from Grey's Anatomy, brought a haunting intensity to his role as the complicated Jacobs patriarch. Losing both of them left a real hole in the cast and the community around the series.

This dedication is not just a sweet gesture. It sets the emotional tone for what is shaping up to be a genuinely heavy season. Euphoria has always been a show about pain, survival and connection. Honoring the people who helped build that world feels exactly right. Sunday cannot come fast enough.
keyPoints: Euphoria Season 3 premieres Sunday April 12 on HBO and Max
Creator Sam Levinson dedicated the season to cast members who have died
Angus Cloud, who played Fez, passed away in July 2023 aged 25
Eric Dane, who played Cal Jacobs, also died before the season aired
The season runs for eight episodes

articles.title: Aubrey Plaza Is Pregnant One Year After Husband's Death
articles.summary: Aubrey Plaza is expecting her first child with a new partner, just over a year after her husband Jeff Baena died in January 2025. This is a genuine moment of hope after one of Hollywood's most heartbreaking losses.
articles.content:
Aubrey Plaza is pregnant. The White Lotus star is expecting her first child with a new partner, and the news is as surprising as it is genuinely moving. This comes just over a year after the death of her husband Jeff Baena, the filmmaker she had been with for over a decade.

Baena passed away in January 2025 at the age of 47. The two had been partners since around 2011 and quietly married before his death. Plaza kept largely private during her grief, so this announcement feels significant. It shows she is building a new chapter for herself.

People have a lot of love for Plaza. She has always been someone who plays things close to the chest, so when news like this comes out it lands differently. Wishing her nothing but good things here.
keyPoints: Aubrey Plaza is pregnant with her first child
She is expecting with a new partner, more than a year after her husband Jeff Baena died
Baena passed away in January 2025 at age 47
Plaza is known for her role in The White Lotus among many other projects
She has stayed largely private since Baena's death

articles.title: Christian Pulisic's Ex Threatens to Expose Cheating Claims
articles.summary: Christian Pulisic's ex-girlfriend is threatening to go public with cheating claims and reveal his alleged dating profile, just weeks before the World Cup. The timing could not be worse for the USA's biggest soccer star.
articles.content:
Christian Pulisic is having a rough week off the pitch. His ex-girlfriend is threatening to expose his alleged dating profile and share details about their relationship, including claims that he was cheating while they were together. Pulisic is the captain and face of the US Men's National Team, so this is landing in a very public way.

The World Cup is coming to North America later this year, with the USA as one of the host nations. Pulisic is expected to be the team's leader and its biggest name. That makes any drama in his personal life instantly bigger news than it would be for most athletes.

So far Pulisic has not responded publicly. His ex has not dropped everything yet either, but the threat alone is enough to get people talking. Whether this turns into a full public falling out or quietly goes away remains to be seen. For now it is a distraction nobody in the US soccer camp wanted heading into the biggest tournament on home soil in decades.
keyPoints: Christian Pulisic's ex-girlfriend is threatening to expose cheating claims
She says she has access to his dating profile as evidence
Pulisic is the star player for the US Men's National Team
The World Cup is coming to North America later in 2026 with the USA as host
Pulisic has not publicly responded to the claims

articles.title: TikTokers Are Speedrunning Scientology Buildings in LA
articles.summary: TikTok creators in Los Angeles are going viral for running into Church of Scientology buildings to see how far they can get before staff remove them. The trend is racking up millions of views and turning an unusual real-world stunt into one of the strangest viral formats of 2026.
articles.content:
Here is the premise. You walk into a Scientology building, camera rolling, and you try to get as deep inside as possible before someone stops you and kicks you out. That is the whole game. TikTokers in LA are calling it speedrunning, borrowing the term from gaming culture where players try to complete a game as fast as possible, and the results are genuinely chaotic and funny.

Scientology is a religious organisation founded by science fiction writer L. Ron Hubbard in the 1950s. It is known for being notoriously secretive and protective of its spaces, which is exactly why this trend works as content. The tension of not knowing what happens next keeps people watching. Some creators get a few steps in. Others make it surprisingly far before staff intervene.

It is silly and it is also a little bit brave. Scientology has a long reputation for aggressive responses to people it sees as antagonists. Whether this trend stays funny or escalates into something more serious is genuinely hard to predict. For now though, it is the internet doing what the internet does best: finding the most unexpected place to have a laugh.
keyPoints: TikTokers in LA are filming themselves running into Scientology buildings
The goal is to get as far inside as possible before being removed
The trend borrows the term speedrunning from gaming culture
Videos are going viral with millions of views
Scientology is known for being secretive and protective of its premises

articles.title: Hinge Now Verifies Your Height and It's Real
articles.summary: A dating app has launched actual height verification as a real feature, letting users prove how tall they are before matches see their profile. This matters because it tackles one of the longest-running complaints in online dating: people lying about their height.
articles.content:
A dating app has officially launched height verification, and yes, it is a real thing. This is not a joke. Back in 2016, Tinder announced height verification as an April Fools prank, poking fun at how many people fib about how tall they are on their profiles. Nearly a decade later, someone actually built it for real.

The feature lets users verify their height so matches can trust what they see on the profile. The app behind it openly admits the move is 'controversial' which honestly makes sense. Some people love the idea of knowing they are getting the truth upfront. Others feel like it adds pressure and turns dating into a checklist. Both sides have a point.

Either way, this is a sign that dating apps are getting more serious about honesty in profiles. Verified photos, verified jobs, now verified heights. The question is where it stops. If this catches on, expect other apps to follow fast.
keyPoints: A dating app has launched real height verification for user profiles
Tinder first teased the idea as an April Fools joke back in 2016
The app itself admits the feature is controversial
It reflects a bigger push for honesty and verification in online dating
Other apps could follow if this feature proves popular
